Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ios): disable New Architecture to resolve startup crash

Native crash on iOS: `attempt to insert nil object from objects[1]`
during Expo module JS object initialization. Occurs when a module's
constantsToExport is called on a background thread (New Architecture
JSI compatibility bridge) before AVFoundation constants are initialized.

Disabling New Arch runs constantsToExport on the main thread where
AVFoundation is fully available. This is a temporary workaround while
expo-av is migrated to expo-audio (expo-av is deprecated in SDK 54).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(docker): e2e pipeline fixes — seccomp, GPU mapping, warning suppression (#585)

* fix(docker): seccomp path resolution + GPU device mapping for post_training/analyze

1. Seccomp path: Docker Compose V2 resolves security_opt paths relative to
   the compose file's directory (deployment/), not the working directory.
   Changed `deployment/seccomp/train-enforce.json` → `seccomp/train-enforce.json`

2. GPU device mapping: post_training (SWA, calibration) and analyze
   (sliding_window_inference) need GPU access. Added CDI device mapping,
   gpu-security (seccomp), and gpu-resources to both services.

3. Warning suppression (DG1.7): Added MetricsReloaded SyntaxWarning,
   ONNX Runtime, CUDA FutureWarning, and MONAI UserWarning suppression
   to train_flow.py entry point.

4. Git hash: Downgraded "Could not determine git hash" from WARNING to
   DEBUG level — expected inside Docker containers without .git.

5. Tests: Added test_compose_seccomp_paths_resolve_from_compose_dir,
   test_gpu_flows_have_cdi_device_mapping, updated GPU service lists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(docker): interpreter-level warning suppression via -W flags (DG1.7)

Python -W flags in Dockerfile.train CMD suppress warnings at interpreter
level, before imports trigger them:
- SyntaxWarning: MetricsReloaded invalid escape sequences (22 lines eliminated)
- FutureWarning: CUDA cudart module deprecation
- UserWarning: MONAI/PyTorch non-tuple indexing (message-based filter for
  C++ triggered warnings where module filter doesn't work)

Also added ORT_LOGGING_LEVEL=3 to docker-compose.flows.yml x-common-env
(ONNX Runtime C++ layer bypasses this for device discovery, but helps
for other ORT warnings).

Result: training output reduced from ~40 noise lines to 3 (1 Docker CLI
orphan warning, 1 ORT C++ device discovery, 1 git hash — all non-Python).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(debug): suppress orphan containers warning via COMPOSE_IGNORE_ORPHANS

Docker Compose V2 reports "Found orphan containers" when infra services
(postgres, minio, mlflow, prefect) from docker-compose.yml share the
"deployment" project name with docker-compose.flows.yml.

COMPOSE_IGNORE_ORPHANS=True suppresses this non-actionable warning.

Training output is now clean: ~45 noise lines reduced to 2 (ORT C++
device discovery, git hash — both non-Python, non-suppressible).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(docker): add DG1.7 warning suppression -W flags to all flow Dockerfiles

All 11 flow Dockerfiles now have Python -W interpreter flags to suppress
non-actionable third-party warnings:
- SyntaxWarning: MetricsReloaded invalid escape sequences
- FutureWarning: CUDA cudart deprecation
- UserWarning: MONAI/PyTorch non-tuple indexing

Added test_flow_dockerfiles_have_warning_suppression to enforce this
for all current and future flow Dockerfiles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Add OpenAI OAuth integration with codex-cli provider support (#7)

* Add codex-cli provider as alternative agent backend

- Add AgentProvider enum ("claude-code" | "codex-cli") to SessionSettings
- Create index-codex.ts entry point using codexAppServer mode with true
  delta streaming via ai-sdk-provider-codex-cli
- Support OAuth token passthrough: CODEX_AUTH_JSON env var is written to
  ~/.codex/auth.json on the VM, with OPENAI_API_KEY as fallback

- Add separate build targets for claude and codex agent bundles

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CpobAvAoiM1tptW6Ske8PP

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CpobAvAoiM1tptW6Ske8PP

* Add per-user OpenAI OAuth PKCE flow for codex-cli provider

- DB migration: openai_tokens table + code_verifier column on oauth_states
- API routes: GET/POST /auth/openai for OAuth PKCE, status, disconnect
- SessionAgentDO: pass userId in init, look up per-user tokens from D1,
  format as auth.json and pass via CODEX_AUTH_JSON env var
- Web app: OpenAI OAuth callback page, useOpenAIAuth hook, Connect
  ChatGPT button on home page
- Falls back to server-wide CODEX_AUTH_JSON if no per-user tokens found

commit.message:
refactor: migrate to model_routes-only configuration

Consolidate all model configuration into model_routes array:
- Remove [openrouter] top-level section
- Add active_route as required field (no default fallback)
- Add Settings::create_provider_from_active_route() method
- Support legacy [openrouter] config with automatic migration
- Model routes can now include per-route temperature, max_tokens, stream

Breaking changes:
- New configs must use [[model_routes]] format
- active_route must be explicitly set (no default)
- config.openrouter.api_key moved to model_routes[].api_key

Backwards compatibility:
- Legacy [openrouter] configs automatically migrated to model_routes
- Migration creates single "default" route from openrouter settings

Example new config format:
```toml
active_route = "fast"

[[model_routes]]
name = "fast"
provider = "openrouter"
model = "gpt-4o-mini"

[[model_routes]]
name = "smart"
provider = "deepseek"
model = "deepseek-chat"
```

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: deduplicate RetellAI webhooks — only process call_analyzed, remove outbound polling

RetellAI sends both call_ended and call_analyzed per call. Processing both
caused duplicate reports and the second task's container could hang, blocking
all messages for the group until the 30-min timeout. Now only call_analyzed
is processed (it includes everything from call_ended plus analysis data).

Also removed the outbound call polling instructions from discord-dm CLAUDE.md
since webhooks now handle both inbound and outbound calls automatically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: add explicit z-index to card faces — front face above flip container

rotateY transform creates a CSS stacking context on the flip container,
causing it to paint above sibling elements regardless of DOM order.
Set z-index:1 on flip container (back face) and z-index:2 on front face
overlay so front face always renders on top when selected.

commit.message:
Fix 13 agent bugs: sweep locks, queue safety, timezone, channel races

- Sweep lock uses job name instead of agent key (no more collisions)
- rejectTask only clears slot it owns, pruneTaskRegistry skips live tasks
- Scheduled handoff uses Intl timezone math instead of naive setHours
- computeEnd uses Date constructor for proper month-end rollover
- Remove \bmeet\b false positive from meeting regex
- Stale lock cleanup clears both channelLocks Set and timestamp Map
- Atomic acquireChannelLock prevents TOCTOU race on channel processing
- Session loader uses absolute paths from import.meta
- Add creative-classify, tm-cookie-refresh, email-check to schedule panel
- Add general webhook profile for #general channel messages

commit.message:
Optimize scraper: block resources, single attempt, one page load

- Remove 3-attempt retry loop (was tripling resource usage on failures)
- Block images, fonts, media, stylesheets via page.route() to cut
  Chromium memory footprint and bandwidth on Vercel
- Eliminate second page navigation to info.do (saves 10-20s and
  a full second browser page); extract match metadata from scorecard
  page instead (CricClubs embeds the same info there)
- Add memory-saving Chromium flags: --disable-background-networking,
  --disable-extensions, --disable-sync, --no-first-run, etc.
- Remove both waitForTimeout(1000) delays (saves 2s)
- Reduce waitForSelector timeout from 20s to 12s
- Reduce goto timeout from 45s to 40s

commit.message:
Fix nginx healthcheck failing due to IPv6 resolution

BusyBox wget resolves localhost to ::1 (IPv6) but nginx only listens
on IPv4. Use 127.0.0.1 explicitly to fix the health check.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: inject sender entity_id into memory_store_fact via routing context [bu-e0g.4] (#529)

When route.execute processes a request with source_sender_entity_id in
the request_context, the entity_id is now injected into _routing_ctx_var
before spawner.trigger() runs. The spawner captures this into the
per-session _runtime_routing_context. memory_store_fact reads
source_entity_id from the runtime session routing context as the default
entity_id when the LLM does not supply one explicitly.

Explicit entity_id from the LLM always takes precedence. Works for both
owner and third-party sender messages.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
